content: Yellow colour of chlorine water fades because of
explanation:
Chlorine water on standing loses yellow colour due to formation of HCl and HOCl which in turns provide nascent oxygen.

$$\mathrm{Cl}_2+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow \mathrm{HCl}+\mathrm{HOCl}$$

$$\mathrm{HClO} \longrightarrow \mathrm{HCl}+$$ $$\mathrm{\mathop {[O]}\limits_{Nascent\,oxygen}}$$

Thus, production of $$\mathrm{HCl}$$ and $$\mathrm{HOCl}$$ are responsible for fading of yellow colour of chlorine water.

content: $$\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$$ is obtained by which of the following?
explanation:
$$\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$$ can be obtained by $$\mathrm{BaO}_2 . 8 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$$ as follows:

$$\mathop {Ba{O_2}.8{H_2}O(s)}\limits_{Barium\,peroxide} + {H_2}S{O_4}(aq)\buildrel {} \over \longrightarrow BaS{O_4}(s) + \mathop {{H_2}{O_2}(aq)}\limits_{Hydrogen\,peroxide} + 8{H_2}O(l)$$

Anhydrous barium oxide is not used here because the precipitate $$\mathrm{BaSO}_4$$ forms a protective layer on the unreacted barium peroxide and this prevents its further participation in the reaction. Hence, option (a) is the correct.
